title: Telemetry Backend — AWS CloudWatch
description: AWS-native observability backend using CloudWatch Metrics, CloudWatch Logs, and AWS X-Ray for distributed tracing. Zero infrastructure to operate; deeply integrated with all AWS services.

cost.costNotes: Free tier: 10 custom metrics, 5GB log ingestion, 100k X-Ray traces/month. Paid: $0.30/custom metric/month, $0.50/GB log ingestion, $0.03/GB log storage, $5/million X-Ray traces. Small (50 metrics, 10GB logs/month): ~$25-40/month. Medium (500 metrics, 100GB logs, 1M traces/month): ~$200-400/month.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: AWS CloudWatch pricing (2025): Custom Metrics $0.30/metric-month (first 10k free), Log Ingestion $0.50/GB, Log Storage $0.03/GB-month, X-Ray Traces $5/million traces (first 100k free). Min ($20): small app, AWS-native services auto-publish free metrics + minimal custom metrics. Max ($1000): medium production with high log volume (500GB/month) and extensive custom metrics.
provenance.adoptionCost: Very low adoption cost — AWS services emit metrics automatically. Custom metrics and log groups require minimal agent configuration (~4h) plus dashboard/alarm setup (~12h). Total ~16h at $30/hour = ~$480.
provenance.licenseCost: No separate license; costs are pay-per-use included in the monthly estimate.

provides.reasoning: AWS CloudWatch is a fully managed observability backend native to AWS. Receives metrics from AWS services automatically and via the CloudWatch agent or OTLP endpoint (via ADOT).
CloudWatch Logs ingests structured JSON logs from Lambda, ECS, EC2, and other services. Supports CloudWatch Logs Insights for structured log querying with field-level extraction.
CloudWatch Metrics stores all metrics as time-series data with configurable resolution (1-second to 1-minute) and 15-month retention, queryable via GetMetricData API and metric math.
AWS X-Ray provides distributed tracing across AWS services and custom applications. Supports the OpenTelemetry SDK via AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry (ADOT) for vendor-neutral instrumentation.

AWS CloudWatch captures and stores discrete application events through CloudWatch Events (EventBridge), custom metrics, and CloudWatch Logs event entries, providing event-tracking infrastructure for compliance, alerting, and operational monitoring.

availability.reason: Available to all AWS accounts; free tier included

supports_nfr.reason: Telemetry backend is meaningful from 90% availability upward; below that, service reliability is the primary concern, not observability infrastructure.
At 4-nines availability, CloudWatch's 1-5 minute metric publish delay may be insufficient for real-time SLO breach detection; sub-minute resolution requires additional tooling.
CloudWatch integrates with AWS CloudTrail for audit logging; compatible with or without formal audit requirements.
CloudWatch is a core AWS managed service; it reconnects automatically after service restarts. RTO >= 5 minutes is comfortably supported.
CloudWatch is a fully managed AWS service that scales automatically; high-throughput workloads are supported natively.
CloudWatch agent collects metrics on a push/pull model asynchronously; it does not add per-request overhead to application read paths.
CloudWatch Logs and X-Ray use asynchronous batched ingestion; they do not constrain application write throughput.
AWS CloudWatch supports GDPR compliance through AWS data residency controls, encryption, and data processing agreements.
AWS CloudWatch is a HIPAA-eligible service included in the AWS BAA; compatible with HIPAA-regulated workloads.
AWS CloudWatch supports CCPA compliance through AWS data privacy controls and deletion capabilities.
CloudWatch integrated with CloudTrail provides the audit trail and access logging needed for SOX compliance.

supports_constraints.reason: CloudWatch is AWS-specific and only available when the target cloud is AWS.
AWS CloudWatch receives telemetry from any language via the CloudWatch agent, AWS SDK, or OTLP (via ADOT).
CloudWatch monitors all AWS-hosted platform types natively.

log_retention_days.description: Log group retention policy. Applied to all log groups created by this pattern. Shorter values reduce CloudWatch Logs storage costs significantly.

xray_sampling_rate.description: X-Ray trace sampling rate (0.05 = 5%). First 100k traces/month free; higher rates increase cost. Use 1.0 only for low-traffic services or debugging.

warn_nfr.reason: At 4-nines availability, CloudWatch metric latency (1-5 minute publish delay) may be insufficient for real-time SLO breach detection.
warn_nfr.message: obs-telemetry-backend--aws-cloudwatch: availability target {actual} (≥99.99%) may require sub-minute metric resolution and alerting; CloudWatch publishes at 1-minute intervals by default.
